item.title: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Edit: timer expired, edits made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

This mentions Gamers Against Weed, but doesn't link to the hub. I propose linking to the hub.

Editing this:

SCP-2562-A possesses a tattoo on its right palm reading "Mr. Top Text, by Gamers Against Weed";

To this:

SCP-2562-A possesses a tattoo on its right palm reading "Mr. Top Text, by Gamers Against Weed";

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Y'know, these guys could be useful with some of the articles on here. Mainly because I am stupid and cannot understand most long articles without looking at the BOTTOM TEXT.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That end of interview feels like a sort of conversation that will happen in the ZF clan. I wonder why no one makes Soviet womble or cyanide into a scip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is one of the memes I somehow missed, but given the simplicity of the conceit (and that it felt like a forced joke even when I didn't get it), I was expecting to downvote. Full disclosure, I kept reading only because I thought it was unfair to not finish due to the writing of the first part being of acceptable quality.

But the interviews solidly won me over. I ended up really liking those two characters. +1'd after all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was surprised that the ending BOTTOM TEXT

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was going to downvote until the interview, where solid characterization took this from being just a one-note gag.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Downvoted. This feels like how I expected most of the GAW Misters to feel - like the names are limiting to creativity, rather than encouraging of it. A predictable gag, well-delivered, didn't do enough for me - this also doesn't give insight into the character(s) of GAW, which was disappointing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No uncollapsed images on the forums, please. -Zyn
hide

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wonder how many pieces I've upvoted because it correctly assumes that I have an understanding of some outside piece of culture. This piece falls flat because it hinges, imo, on familiarity with a joke I have never seen. I can only infer why the joke is supposed to be funny, but that ruins the joke. It would be like someone wrote an English language article that was just about Mr. Grass Mud Horse and the joke was "it involves swearing!"

As always, solidly written. But yeah, -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I mean, I was one of the chat reviewers and I was hoping for something interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My apologies; I was overly demanding. I didn't understand how involved kinch was in GAW canon, and thought you'd overlooked it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mr. Top Text makes implicit information explicit. In the example provided, it makes a common point about how the traveler is a framing device that serves to distance and diminish Ozymandias's power. It diminishes how good the actual writing is, but if someone wasn't twigging onto the purpose of the traveler in the poem, it would be nice to have someone point it out.

I cared less about making it interesting in its own right than I did about having it be a good illustration of the effects, because that section was only added at the behest of chat reviewers, and this is what I got the impression of them wanting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I suppose that they are meant to be uninteresting. My perception of Mr. Top Text's abilities is that they spoil the material for you, but what it actually does seems to be further trolling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2562-B: Oh, well then I'm bored. Conversation's over. Bottom text.
Dr. Steele: You can't skip real-life conversations by saying "bottom text".
SCP-2562-B: Bottom text.

Goddammit, I laughed a lot at this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I expected to not like this given the conceit, and I'm still not walking away wanting to upvote. However, I do think this avoided being simply an obvious interpretation of the whole "BOTTOM TEXT" meme, and it's yet again another good way to evoke character from these anomalies, so I don't dislike it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Go to the image url and refresh that page. That should fix the image on your end.

I addressed the matter of whether they get the consciousnesses from (tl;dr: they rent out souls to stick into meat shells for a while) elsewhere, but I haven't addressed, in any piece, where they get the human bodies. I generally assume that they are magicked into existence out of whole cloth.

My personal headcanon is that magically summoning a human body is actually extremely easy if you know how, because so much of this world has been adapted to incorporate humans readily, and so much of human consciousness is focused on other humans.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Solid piece. By the way, the mugshot is broken.

I'm rather curious how GAW can just pop humans out of thin air. Anomalous humans, but still fully-sapient as far as I can tell. At least the Little Misters seemed to be transformed humans. GAW is either making humans out of nothing, making really accurate fake humans, rewriting old humans into the Misters like Wondertainment (which feels like it goes against their mostly-pacifist stance since it practically kills the prior person), or BOTTOM TEXT.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just answered this in the Gamers Against Weed hub, essentially confirming kinch's answer.

Was it really necessary to post in both places about it? I don't like to make posts just directing people to another post, and I don't like leaving legitimate concerns unanswered, so you've put me in an awkward position here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, I'm going to ask again because I'm still curious, and would like an answer from the author: Why is GAW making sapient anomalies for their amusement, and letting them fall into Foundation hands, without any evidence that this was consensual on the part of the people they either altered or created from scratch? Doesn't that basically go against everything they claim to stand for?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I found the poem quite unimpressive. Those edits are very minor and uninteresting. Mostly though, the article doesn't speak to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice. The interviews sold it for me.

Something interesting, every single Mister Against Weed so far is in the employ of the Foundation to some degree. I have no problem with that, since they're pretty benign (so far) and they're still contained, but I think it could set up some very interesting tales in the future.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That punchline… It reminds me of people who actually say "hashtag" in real life (which makes me resent the concept of hashtag as a whole).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The article's already fairly lengthy for what it is, so I'll just leave out the context for the interviews, since it's not relevant. As for the video games, I imagine that's covered under "standard amenities".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is so dumb but you set it up so well. +1, my only quibble is wishing there was more context for the interview logs but that's such a minor thing as to be irrelevant. Also -B mentions getting time to play video games, maybe include that in the containment procedures?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The bottom text punchline at the end of the interview almost made me choke on my ginger ale.

item.title: Thanks to my pal Vee who inspired the idea, and thanks to everyone who looked over
item.content:encoded: BOTTOM TEXT

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
first post
BOTTOM TEXT
